resume_policy.priority_rule: Lower numeric priority values are handled first.
resume_policy.dependency_rule: Do not start a planned feature until all dependencies are complete.
resume_policy.evidence_rule: A feature can be marked complete only when evidence contains concrete files, commands, logs, metrics, or generated artifacts.

features.name: Project agent and Codex guidance docs
acceptance: Provide canonical project-level agent instructions.
Document deletion safety rules and code boundaries.
Document project commands, structure, conventions, and working style for Codex.
Provide a compatibility AGENT.md that points to AGENTS.md.
features.notes: AGENTS.md is canonical; AGENT.md is only a compatibility pointer.

features.name: Pretraining data mix v1
next_steps: Use this mix as input when implementing the pretraining data planner/sampler.
acceptance: Define a Chinese-priority bilingual token-based pretraining mix whose bucket weights sum to 100.
Document data rules for cleaned, deduplicated, quality-filtered, tokenized corpus mixing.
Record stage guidance for broad pretraining, knowledge/reasoning continuation, and long-context continuation.
Provide a validation script that checks the machine-readable mix configuration.
features.notes: First default mix; intended to be adjusted after evaluation results.

features.name: Python package scaffold
next_steps: Create pyproject.toml for Python 3.11 with pytest and ruff.
Create tests skeleton and a minimal import test.
Add global validation commands to CODEX.md after they exist.
acceptance: Project has an installable Python package under src/tinyllm.
pytest can run at least one minimal test.
ruff can check the repository without rewriting files.
CODEX.md documents the new global commands.
features.notes: Installable package scaffold and global validation commands are now present.

features.name: Qwen3-like architecture specification
next_steps: Create model architecture documentation for 64M trial and 120M-130M MVP configs.
Define config fields for RMSNorm, RoPE, GQA, QK norm, SwiGLU, tied embeddings, and context length.
acceptance: Architecture doc defines the model family and initial size configs.
Config fields are precise enough to implement without new design decisions.
Architecture choices remain HF-compatible.
features.notes: Architecture spec and initial model configs are now implemented and validated.

features.name: Pretraining data pipeline specification
next_steps: Document raw input formats, normalized document schema, filtering stages, dedup stages, tokenization, packing, sharding, and manifests.
Define required metadata fields for provenance, license, cleaning version, token counts, and filter statistics.
Define validation commands for pipeline configs.
acceptance: Pipeline spec describes end-to-end data flow from raw documents to tokenized shards.
Manifest and metadata requirements are explicit.
The spec references the pretraining mix config without duplicating its content.
features.notes: Keep data mix details in data docs/configs, not global guidance files.

features.name: Pretraining loop specification
next_steps: Document training entrypoints, config layout, DDP launch, logging, checkpointing, resume, and smoke tests.
Define minimum validations for tiny synthetic data and one real mini-shard.
acceptance: Spec defines the pretraining loop behavior and required commands.
Checkpoint/resume and logging requirements are explicit.
Smoke-test criteria are explicit.
features.notes: Implementation should wait until model and data specs are stable.
